name: Axial Helix Antenna (wire)
description: A helical antenna operating in axial mode where the helix circumference is approximately one wavelength, producing circularly polarized radiation along the helix axis. The antenna consists of a wire wound in a helical coil above a ground plane, providing excellent circular polarization purity and moderate gain.

circumference.name: Helix Circumference
circumference.description: Circumference of the helical coil, typically close to one wavelength for axial mode operation

pitch_angle.name: Pitch Angle
pitch_angle.description: Angle between the helix wire and a plane perpendicular to the helix axis

spacing.name: Turn Spacing
spacing.description: Axial distance between adjacent turns of the helix

num_turns.name: Number of Turns
num_turns.description: Total number of helical turns, affects gain and bandwidth

wire_diameter.name: Wire Diameter
wire_diameter.description: Diameter of the wire used to construct the helix

ground_plane_diameter.name: Ground Plane Diameter
ground_plane_diameter.description: Diameter of the ground plane reflector

designMethodology.overview: Design process for axial mode helical antenna involves determining helix dimensions for desired frequency, calculating number of turns for required gain, and optimizing impedance matching.

steps.title: Determine Operating Frequency and Wavelength
steps.description: Calculate free space wavelength at the center frequency

steps.title: Calculate Helix Circumference
steps.description: Set circumference close to one wavelength for axial mode operation

steps.title: Determine Turn Spacing and Pitch Angle
steps.description: Calculate spacing for optimal circular polarization, typically λ/4

steps.title: Calculate Number of Turns
steps.description: Determine turns based on desired gain and bandwidth requirements

steps.title: Design Ground Plane
steps.description: Size ground plane for proper reflection and pattern control

steps.title: Calculate Input Impedance
steps.description: Estimate impedance for matching network design

steps.title: Optimize Wire Diameter
steps.description: Select wire diameter for mechanical stability and bandwidth
steps.formula: a = λ/100 to λ/50 for good bandwidth

mockSolverHints.impedanceModel: Use transmission line model with helical geometry corrections and ground plane effects
mockSolverHints.radiationModel: Apply Hansen-Woodyard condition with helical current distribution and axial mode assumptions
keyAssumptions: Axial mode operation with C ≈ λ

references: Balanis, C.A., 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design', 4th Edition, Chapter 10
Stutzman, W.L. and Thiele, G.A., 'Antenna Theory and Design', 3rd Edition, Chapter 8
Kraus, J.D., 'Antennas for All Applications', 3rd Edition, Chapter 7
Volakis, J.L., 'Antenna Engineering Handbook', 4th Edition, Chapter 5
